message:
fix(service): panic-safe state machines for filter, load_balance, load_shed

Rewrite Future::poll implementations across three service middleware
layers to use std::mem::replace for move-based state transitions,
preventing state corruption when inner futures panic during polling.

FilterFuture (src/service/filter.rs):
- Temporarily transitions to Done state via replace before polling
  inner future; restores previous state only on Poll::Pending
- Adds panic test verifying the filter recovers gracefully

LoadBalancedFuture (src/service/load_balance.rs):
- Introduces LoadMetricPollGuard RAII wrapper to ensure in-flight load
  metrics are released even if the inner future panics or is dropped
- Rewrites poll to use guard during polling
- Adds three tests: panic path, pending restoration, load metric
  lifecycle

LoadShedFuture (src/service/load_shed.rs):
- Same replace-based state machine pattern as filter
- Adds panic test

Without these fixes, a panic during poll would leave the state machine
in an inconsistent state, and subsequent panic-recovery attempts could
double-poll a consumed future or leak load metrics that permanently
inflate the load balancer's view of in-flight requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
